title: Mathematics + Financial Literacy: ICSE Class 9 — Compound Interest and Statistics
subject: Mathematics

questions.text: A car depreciates at 15% per year. If the current value is ₹8,00,000, what will it be worth after 3 years?
questions.explanation: Depreciation uses CI formula: A = P(1 - r/100)^t = 800000(0.85)^3 = 800000×0.614125 = ₹4,91,300 ≈ ₹4,89,050. Car depreciation is why financial advisors say: 'a car is a liability, not an asset'.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.text: A company's annual profits (in lakhs) for 5 years: 12, 18, 14, 25, 11. Calculate mean and median. Which should a job-seeker use to judge company stability?

options.text: Mean=16 lakhs, Median=14 lakhs; Median is better as it's resistant to outliers
Mean=16 lakhs, Median=18 lakhs; Mean shows total earning
Mean=14 lakhs, Median=16 lakhs; Mean is always better
Mean=20 lakhs, Median=14 lakhs; use mode for stability

questions.explanation: Sorted: 11,12,14,18,25. Mean = 80/5 = 16. Median = 14 (middle value). For stability, median is better — it ignores the one exceptional year (25 lakhs) that might not repeat. Analysts use both for a complete picture.
questions.subject: Mathematics
